explicit_text: My grandfather, Arthur Kohlstrunk, grew up in Boonton, New Jersey, as the son of Walter and Olga. He often joked about how the family names became more Americanized with each generation. Arthur shared many stories about his childhood in a diverse immigrant neighborhood and wished he could remember more German to connect with his roots.

explicit_text: Walter Kohlstrunk, my great-grandfather, immigrated to the United States from Germany in 1913 under unusual circumstances. He had served in the German army from 1910 to 1912 but hated it and left to work as a steward on a steamship. When World War I broke out, he was docked in New York Harbor and chose not to return to Germany, effectively starting a new life in New York City and later Boonton, New Jersey.

Sadly, this meant Walter never saw his family in Germany again.

explicit_text: Boonton, New Jersey, was where my grandfather Arthur Kohlstrunk and his family lived after settling in the United States. It was a small town with a rich mix of immigrants from Germany, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Italy. My grandfather loved telling stories about growing up there, including how the neighborhood was a true melting pot and the funny mix-up involving a coal truck and the family name.
